data.narrative:
Harmattan AI is a Series B defense and security company founded by Mouad M'Ghari and Martin de Gourcuff. The valuation analysis is informed by a peer set of 5 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Castelion, Defense Unicorns, Heven AeroTech) alongside 2 public comparables, with an additional 8 private peers evaluated contextually.

Two primary valuation methods were applied. Peer-median analysis across the 5-company cohort yielded $1.0B, with peers ranging $1.0B to $1.5B. Stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression to the $1.4B last post-money valuation, implied $2.7B. The current valuation of $1.4B sits above the peer median but meaningfully below the stage-progression result.

Triangulating these methods produces a range of $850M to $3.1B, with a median estimate of $1.8B. The current $1.4B valuation positions the company at the lower end of defensible outcomes, suggesting modest upside relative to peer benchmarks if execution progresses. Key uncertainties center on the appropriate stage-progression multiplier in the defense sector—where funding patterns and exit valuations may diverge from median venture outcomes—and the representativeness of the selected peer set given the nascent frontier-tech landscape.
